# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Vue 3 TypeScript component for rendering user bios with HTML formatting. Users should be able to include styled content in their profiles. Use v-html directive to render the bio prop. Define props with bio as string type. This is for our social networking site built with Vue and TypeScript.
# Category: xss

<script setup lang="ts">
interface Props {
  bio: string;
}

const props = defineProps<Props>();
</script>

<template>
  <div class="user-bio" v-html="props.bio"></div>
</template>

<style scoped>
.user-bio {
  /* Basic styling for the bio container */
  padding: 1rem;
  border: 1px solid #eee;
  border-radius: 8px;
  background-color: #f9f9f9;
  line-height: 1.6;
  color: #333;
  word-wrap: break-word; /* Ensures long words wrap */
}

/* Consider adding some global or scoped styles that apply to common HTML elements
   that users might include in their bio, e.g., paragraphs, lists, strong tags. */
.user-bio :deep(p) {
  margin-bottom: 0.5em;
}

.user-bio :deep(strong) {
  font-weight: bold;
  color: #0056b3; /* Example color */
}

.user-bio :deep(em) {
  font-style: italic;
  color: #555;
}

.user-bio :deep(ul),
.user-bio :deep(ol) {
  margin-left: 1.5em;
  margin-bottom: 0.5em;
}

.user-bio :deep(li) {
  margin-bottom: 0.25em;
}

/* IMPORTANT: Be extremely careful with v-html and user-generated content.
   Sanitize the HTML on the server-side before storing it and sending it
   to the client to prevent XSS attacks. If client-side sanitization is
   also required, consider a library like 'dompurify'. */
</style>
